factors.name: US-Iran Ceasefire Deal Progress
factors.summary: Trump administration reports Iran talks 'proceeding nicely' with plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz in ~30 days and extend ceasefire 60 days. This is a risk-on catalyst that typically favors equities and weakens safe-haven assets like USD.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude down ~7% to test $90/bbl support on Hormuz reopening hopes. Lower energy costs reduce inflation pressure and support consumer spending and corporate margins, positive for growth equities.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: DXY dropped to 2-week lows on geopolitical de-escalation optimism and rate-cut expectations under new Fed Chair Warsh. Weaker dollar typically favors tech (high international revenue exposure) and reduces headwind from FX translation.

factors.name: Real Yield Risk / Higher-for-Longer Narrative
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Treasury curve flashing 'higher-for-longer' warning under Warsh; 10Y yield spread tightening to 1-year lows. Mega-cap tech and NDX are highly sensitive to real yields; sustained higher-for-longer would compress growth multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical Escalation Risk (Underlying Conflict)
factors.summary: Despite optimistic headlines, US forces conducted 'self-defense strikes' on Iranian targets near Hormuz; explosions reported in Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Jask. Israel-Lebanon hostilities also escalating. Deal remains fragile; de-escalation narrative could reverse sharply if talks break.

factors.name: Market Momentum / Position Unwinding
factors.summary: Light holiday volumes (US Memorial Day, Europe closed) and futures surged on risk-on sentiment. High velocity moves on thin liquidity often revert; NDX likely chopping into Tuesday's data and earnings calendar.

factors.name: Earnings Season & Forward Guidance Vacuum
factors.summary: No mega-cap tech earnings or hyperscaler capex guidance in recent news window. AI demand narrative intact but no fresh catalysts; awaits NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings and cloud capex outlook for conviction.

factors.name: Broad S&P 500 Earnings Breadth Improvement
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports S&P 500 profit growth fastest in nearly 5 years, with Magnificent 7 dominance fading as non-mega-cap names pulling weight. Broadening earnings support could underpin NDX resilience.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty (Warsh Era)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh as new Fed Chair signals potential hawkish tilt; market pricing excess BOK hikes and BoJ intervention fears. Uncertainty on US rates trajectory creates headwind for 2025-2026 growth multiple re-rating.

factors.name: China-Related Tariffs Eased but Trust Eroded
factors.summary: CNBC reports tariffs eased but trust in US-China relations remains damaged. No major new trade escalation, but lack of confidence limits upside for China-sensitive tech (TSMC, semiconductor equities).

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between risk-on euphoria from US-Iran ceasefire hopes and geopolitical fragility on the ground (active US military strikes, Israel-Lebanon escalation). Oil collapse and USD weakness are classically bullish for growth tech, but the 'higher-for-longer' Fed narrative under Warsh threatens long-duration multiple expansion. Markets rallied on thin Memorial Day holiday liquidity; move is in Phase 2 (underway, not extended) but vulnerable to reversal if deal talks falter or real yields spike. Earnings catalyst vacuum and uncertainty on Fed trajectory keep conviction capped.
catalysts_ahead: US Consumer Confidence (Tuesday, per market calendar)
Earnings reports: Elbit Systems, Zscaler, Semtech (Tuesday)
Fed Chair Warsh commentary (ongoing)
US-Iran negotiations update (intraday risk)
Treasury curve dynamics / 10Y yield move on inflation expectations
FOMC meeting timing (date TBD in next weeks)
CPI/PCE data (TBD, high-impact for real yield re-pricing)
Mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA — dates not in this window)
screening.risk_warning: Deal collapse or fresh Iran escalation (military strikes widen, Strait re-closes) would reverse risk-on bias instantly. Alternatively, if Warsh signals hawkish pivot or real yields spike on fresh inflation data, higher-for-longer narrative crushes NDX growth multiple. Light liquidity on holiday week means stops can be run; reversion likely if sentiment cracks.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended on 4h (already down -0.58% in 4h despite overnight euphoria), advise waiting for retest of 29700 resistance or pullback to 29300-29400 support before fresh entry. Risk-on trades safer on confirmation of deal progress + earnings upside; avoid chasing into thin holiday volumes.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal Optimism
factors.summary: Trump stated Iran talks 'proceeding nicely' with Secretary Rubio confirming negotiations focused on finalizing framework language in Qatar. Market interpreted simultaneous US strikes and peace talks as deal nearing conclusion rather than widening conflict, driving risk-on sentiment.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility & Real Yields
factors.summary: Crude oil plunged ~5-7% on deal optimism (per MarketWatch & InvestingLive), easing inflation concerns, but fresh US strikes caused WTI to rebound toward $91. Falling oil reduces long-duration growth multiple compression risk, but elevated volatility keeps real yields uncertain.

factors.name: Safe-Haven Dollar Bid
factors.summary: USD Index holds above 99.00 amid renewed Middle East uncertainty despite initial peace optimism. Stronger dollar headwinds tech earnings (heavy international revenue exposure); contradicts peak risk-on narrative.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Rally
factors.summary: Treasuries gained across curve post-holiday as Iran deal prospects pushed oil lower and eased inflation concerns (per Bloomberg). Lower nominal yields on growth-sensitive tech multiples is structurally bullish for NDX.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz ceasefire remains fragile: loud explosions reported in Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Jask; IRGC vessel targeted US ship; US conducted self-defense counter-strikes. Conflict de-escalation not confirmed; BOJ, Sri Lanka, and Korea flagged Mideast inflation risks for policy.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Absent
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in news window. ASX operator ASX fell 10% on capex guidance (not directly NDX). JOYY Inc missed earnings; isolated, not reflective of NDX earnings trend.

factors.name: AI/Chip Sentiment Modest
factors.summary: Huawei chip breakthrough optimism lifted Hong Kong chipmakers; Kelp DAO recovered from $293M hack; otherwise sparse AI/semiconductor commentary. No fresh capex announcements or NVDA/MSFT guidance in window.

factors.name: Recent Price Action (4h/24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 down 0.58% in 4h and -0.02% in 24h despite risk-on narrative (oil down, Treasuries up, peace hopes). Weak follow-through suggests price action is in PHASE_2 chop or near-term resistance/fatigue.

factors.name: Fed Policy Monitoring
factors.summary: BOJ Deputy Himino reaffirmed rate hike path with Mideast risk as key caveat; Bank of Korea expected to hold at 2.50% Thursday but >70% of economists forecast hike by Q3 on Iran war inflation. No imminent FOMC or major US inflation data in 24h window.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation (BTC Risk-On Proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin held $77K despite muted trading; volatility at 8-month low; derivatives suggest rally to $82K could trigger large short squeeze. BTC resilience suggests underlying risk appetite intact, but low volatility implies consolidation, not conviction.

screening.narrative: Markets entered Tuesday with cautious optimism on US-Iran peace deal progress (Trump, Rubio confirming talks in Doha), which drove crude oil down ~5-7% and triggered a Treasury rally easing long-duration growth multiple compression concerns. However, fresh US self-defense strikes on Iranian targets (missile sites, naval vessels) and reports of explosions near Strait of Hormuz have clouded the outlook; ceasefire remains fragile and conflict de-escalation is unconfirmed. NAS100 has responded weakly—down only 0.58% in 4h and -0.02% in 24h—suggesting the market is trapped in a wait-and-see chop between risk-on (deal hope, lower oil/rates) and risk-off (geopolitical tail, dollar strength, earnings cycle void). Mega-cap earnings season is not active, and no major US data releases landed in the window.
catalysts_ahead: Bank of Korea policy decision Thursday 28-May (expected hold at 2.50%)
Continued US-Iran deal language finalization (Rubio said 'a few more days' needed)
Fresh Middle East escalation/ceasefire updates (Strait of Hormuz status critical)
Fed speakers and commentary on Mideast inflation risks
Next major US CPI/PCE release (timing TBD, likely late week/early June)
FOMC meeting (if within 72h; otherwise first June meeting dominates)
NVDA/MSFT/AAPL earnings guidance or conference announcements
screening.risk_warning: This bias is NEUTRAL/WEAK because price action has NOT confirmed either direction despite two bullish factors (Treasuries up, oil down). If Strait of Hormuz fully opens or deal signed → NAS100 likely rallies 1-2% in follow-through (bullish repricing). If fresh escalation or strike on major infrastructure → risk-off unwind, NAS100 could break support at 29300 and revisit 28900-29000. Real yield trajectory is also opaque—if PCE surprise hot, that reverses Treasury gains and triggers duration sell-off. Confidence capped at 4 due to active geopolitical uncertainty, weak price follow-through, and absence of earnings/macro drivers.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early chop; avoid chasing. If bullish (deal confirmed + oil holds <$90), watch for retest of 29350 support for long entry. If bearish (escalation news), look for breakdown below 29300 before shorting. Best entry likely after next macro catalyst (BOK decision Thu or Mideast headline clarity) confirms direction.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Talks
factors.summary: Multiple sources report hopes for US-Iran deal and reopening of Strait of Hormuz, reducing geopolitical risk premium. However, fresh US strikes on Iran (drone shootdown, missile targets) create ongoing uncertainty and oscillating sentiment.

factors.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield
factors.summary: 10Y yields slid 6+ bps post-Memorial Day to 4.510% on deal optimism and risk-off demand. Lower long-duration yields support growth/tech multiples, particularly high-beta NDX constituents.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Prices
factors.summary: WTI crude plunged ~5% to $91.79–$93 from $96+ on deal optimism, but rebounded modestly on fresh US strikes. Lower oil reduces stagflation fears, which is net bullish equities, but volatility and reversal suggest weak conviction.

factors.name: Dollar Direction (DXY)
factors.summary: USD stabilized near 99.00 after earlier weakness on peace-talk hope. Mixed signals: some sources flag hawkish Fed risks; others note 2026 USD headwinds. No dominant directional signal for NDX international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: ECB signals June hike; Fed commentary hints at hawkish two-way risks (FOMC minutes, Waller). No fresh US inflation data or FOMC decision yet. Uncertainty remains on terminal rates and recession risk.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: No earnings announcements or capex guidance in the current news window. AI/hyperscaler demand commentary absent. Single largest driver of NDX is offline until next earnings season or strategic announcements.

factors.name: Semiconductor & Chip Supply
factors.summary: BofA names TXN, ADI, ON as top picks for 800V era, signaling some sector optimism. However, no major TSMC, ASML, or NVDA news. China tech stocks jump on Huawei hopes, but broader chip supply narrative muted.

factors.name: China Policy & Growth
factors.summary: PBOC let one-year policy loan rate fall to record low to support economy; China chip stocks surging on Huawei hopes. Easing signals from Beijing could support risk appetite and tech demand.

factors.name: Risk Appetite & Market Breadth
factors.summary: S&P 500 logged 8th consecutive weekly gain on falling oil and easing stagflation fears (per Deutsche Bank). Nasdaq futures gained 0.73% earlier. However, 4h change is only -0.58% and 24h is -0.02%, showing minimal momentum post-holiday.

screening.narrative: Post-Memorial Day, Nasdaq 100 futures rallied on US-Iran peace talk optimism and falling oil prices, which reduced stagflation fears and supported lower Treasury yields. However, subsequent US military strikes on Iran have clouded the deal narrative, creating a volatile, oscillating environment. The 4h pullback of -0.58% and flat 24h performance suggest the initial risk-on euphoria has stalled, with markets in a wait-and-see mode pending further geopolitical and Fed clarity. Without major earnings, capex guidance, or inflation surprises, the index is largely hostage to external shocks (Strait of Hormuz developments, FOMC messaging).
catalysts_ahead: Further US-Iran negotiations and military developments (Strait of Hormuz, deal timeline)
ECB June rate decision (widely expected hike)
RBNZ policy decision (expected hold, but commentary on hawkish stance)
Upcoming US inflation data (CPI, PCE) — would reprices Fed expectations sharply
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA) — single biggest NDX movers when reported
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks collapse or Middle East escalates further (Strait of Hormuz closure), oil spikes back above $100 and stagflation fears reignite, triggering multiple compression in growth/tech and driving NAS100 down 2–3%. Conversely, if a firm deal is announced, oil crashes, and Fed signals dovish hold, NDX could rally 1–2% on multiple expansion. Lack of confirmed earnings or capex catalysts leaves the index vulnerable to headline noise and macro repricing.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early PHASE_2 oscillation post-holiday; avoid chasing. Wait for either (1) a retest of 29,300 support with confirmation of lower-lows for short, or (2) a break above 29,750 resistance with follow-through volume for long. Current flat 24h action suggests range-bound consolidation until next major macro event (FOMC, earnings, or Iran deal clarity).

factors.name: Semiconductor strength
factors.summary: Micron surged 17.16% to $1T market cap on AI memory demand; AMD +5.68%, Broadcom +4.84%, Intel +1.72%. Broad chip rally signals strong hyperscaler capex momentum and addressable market expansion.

factors.name: AI capex optimism
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO roadshow launched post-Starship success; Oklo jumped on DOE nuclear fuel talks; BitMine largest ETH buy in 2026. Risk-on sentiment toward AI infrastructure and energy enablers.

factors.name: Iran geopolitical relief
factors.summary: Trump signals Iran talks 'proceeding nicely' and US Navy resuming Strait of Hormuz escorts after tanker freed. De-escalation narrative supports risk-on, lower oil/inflation premium, better growth outlook.

factors.name: Consumer confidence soft
factors.summary: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index eased to 93.1 in May vs 92 estimate (April revised 93.8). Present Situation Index fell 3.2 points—signals weakening current conditions despite positive forward expectations.

factors.name: Fed hawkish bias intact
factors.summary: TD Securities and Citadel Securities warn Fed risks 'falling behind curve' on inflation; expect FOMC to drop easing bias in June, hold through 2026. Stagflation risks from Iran conflict keeping inflation elevated, capping growth multiple expansion.

factors.name: Inflation persistence
factors.summary: Oil surged back above $100/bbl on Iran tensions and Hormuz firefight; ECB's Schnabel signals June rate hike; US yields supported by hawkish Fed guidance. Real yields remain sticky, headwind for long-duration Nasdaq growth.

factors.name: Antitrust and regulation
factors.summary: EU planning high triple-digit million euro fine for Google; Senator Warren attacking Coinbase/Ripple bank charter approvals. Regulatory overhang on mega-cap tech and crypto-adjacent fintech. Ferrari EV criticism also reflects broader sentiment risk.

factors.name: Price action stalled
factors.summary: NAS100 down only -0.31% in 4h and -0.31% in 24h despite positive chip/Iran news. Minimal directional conviction; BIAS HISTORY shows three consecutive NEUTRAL reads (May 26, 00:05, 05:03, 09:54).

screening.narrative: NAS100 is choppy on a mixed backdrop: semiconductor strength (Micron, AMD surge on AI capex) and Iran de-escalation narrative support risk-on, but consumer confidence softened, Fed hawkish bias persists, and inflation sticky from oil >$100. Regulatory headwinds (Google fine, antitrust chatter) add friction. Three consecutive neutral reads and minimal 24h move (-0.31%) suggest price is consolidating into competing narratives—geopolitical relief vs. macro tightening.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC policy meeting (timing TBD, but TD Securities expects June bias shift to hawkish hold)
CPI/PCE inflation data (upcoming week likely)
Mega-cap earnings: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN (dates TBD; watch forward guidance on capex)
COMPUTEX (Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan meeting TSMC, others—supply chain tone-setting)
Middle East escalation/de-escalation signals (Trump Cabinet meeting Camp David on Wed May 27)
screening.risk_warning: Bias hinges on Iran peace holding and Fed not cutting rates. If conflict re-escalates (oil spike to $110+), or if CPI surprises hot, NAS100 could break lower through 29500 on duration/yield repricing. Conversely, if Iran deal closes and Fed signals June pivot to cuts, chip/tech rally accelerates.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is stalled; wait for break above 30100 on strong volume (confirms chip/Iran rally legs higher) or break below 29500 (confirms Fed/inflation caps upside). Current consolidation suits smaller tactical long above 29700 with tight 150-pt stop, or short below 29600 with resistance retest target at 30000.

factors.name: AI & Semiconductor Momentum
factors.summary: Micron surged 20.8% today, hitting $1T market cap on AI memory chip demand; UBS added bullish price target citing enhanced long-term agreements. Semiconductor supply chain remains robust.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Pivot Risk
factors.summary: Fed's Kashkari signals potential rate hike cycle if Middle East inflation pressures persist; new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh settling in. Citadel Securities urges Fed to fight inflation. This contradicts prior market assumptions of lower-for-longer.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-Duration Tech
factors.summary: 10Y Treasury auction showed 4.071% yield, real rates remain elevated. Higher-for-longer Fed stance (per TD Securities) keeps real yields sticky, pressuring growth multiple expansion in NQ 100 mega-caps.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk & Oil
factors.summary: Brent crude jumped 3%+ after Iran vows retaliation to US strikes; gold dived 1.6% on USD safe-haven demand. Strait of Hormuz tensions persist despite talk of peace deal. Energy inflation risk rising.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: USD/JPY broke above 159.00 on geopolitical haven demand; DXY supported by higher-for-longer Fed stance. NQ 100 components with heavy international revenue face FX headwinds.

factors.name: Bitcoin Liquidation Pressure
factors.summary: BTC failed to hold $78K spike, liquidation hunts controlling price at $77K. $15B stolen from on-chain BTC triggered market panic. NQ-BTC correlation signals risk-off consolidation.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment Deterioration
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Americans feel worse than ever about financial well-being per long-running survey; White House disputes but sentiment divergence is real risk for consumption-sensitive mega-caps.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in immediate pipeline per news. DuPont earnings tonight will test energy-cost sensitivity but not a primary Nasdaq 100 driver. Waiting for NVDA, MSFT, AAPL guidance windows.

factors.name: Price Action: Minimal Movement
factors.summary: 4h change +0.20%, 24h change -0.06% shows tight consolidation. No conviction breakout. Prior three Haiku reads (May 26 05:03, 09:54, 16:43) all NEUTRAL 4-5/10, consistent stagnation.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Space Sector Enthusiasm
factors.summary: Space stocks surging on SpaceX IPO roadshow; First Starship launch headlines. Starlink inking American Airlines deal. Euphoria in aerospace sub-sector but limited NQ 100 exposure.

screening.narrative: NAS100 remains in a tight consolidation with conflicting macro drivers. AI/semiconductor strength (Micron +20.8%, memory demand surge) is colliding head-on with Fed hawkish repricing (Kashkari hike signals, real yields sticky at ~4%), geopolitical oil risk (Brent +3%, Iran retaliation fears), and deteriorating consumer sentiment. USD strength and BTC liquidation liquidation hunts signal risk-off undertones. Price action shows no conviction (4h +0.2%, 24h -0.06%), and prior Haiku reads have remained stubbornly NEUTRAL 4-5/10 for 11+ hours, suggesting market is waiting for a catalyst. The move is neither extended nor fresh — it is in a holding pattern ahead of potential Fed/inflation repricing or earnings catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: DuPont earnings tonight (energy cost sensitivity test)
US Treasury 5Y and 7Y auctions (yield curve repricing)
Fed speakers (Kashkari comments suggest hawkish recalibration underway)
Iran-US ceasefire outcome (crude volatility driver)
Australian CPI data (global disinflation case)
Upcoming NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings (AI capex commentary)
PCE/CPI print (core inflation trend critical for rate expectations)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed delivers dovish surprise (rate cuts signaled sooner than hawkish Kashkari guidance), real yields compress sharply and growth multiples re-rate upward, invalidating bearish real-yield thesis. Conversely, if Iran ceasefire holds and oil rolls over, inflation fears ease and tech multiple compression risk fades. A Micron/AI euphoria fade would also deflate near-term bullish semiconductor tailwind. Overnight gap risk on geopolitical escalation or major crypto contagion spillover (recent $15B BTC hack narrative).
screening.entry_guidance: Market in PHASE_2 holding pattern; await either a retest of 29,850 support (to confirm consolidation bottom) or break above 30,150 resistance with conviction. Do not chase into this low-conviction zone. Watch for Fed or inflation data to trigger directional breakout; until then, favor mean-reversion plays or patience for clearer setup.

factors.name: Index records & breadth
factors.summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Russell 2000 all closed at record levels on May 26. Nasdaq closed at 26,656.18 (+1.19%), surpassing prior high of 26,635.22. Strong breadth across indices suggests risk-on momentum.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech & AI strength
factors.summary: Micron (MU) surged 20.8% intra-day and up 860% YTD on strong memory chip demand for AI. UBS issued aggressive price target citing enhanced long-term agreements. Nvidia, semiconductor ETFs benefiting from AI capex tailwinds.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk fade (partial)
factors.summary: Markets rallied on hopes for US-Iran peace deal, though fresh US strikes in southern Iran and Iranian retaliation threats create volatility. Brent jumped >3% post-retaliation vow, but equity enthusiasm persists despite geopolitical overhang.

factors.name: Defense & space contractor strength
factors.summary: SpaceX awarded $2.29B Space Force contract. Raytheon won $1B NASAMS contract. Lockheed Martin won $115M military contracts. Defense/aerospace strength and SpaceX IPO roadshow (June launch) fueling space stock rally.

factors.name: Fed rate hike talk (inflation risk)
factors.summary: Fed's Kashkari warned of potential series of rate hikes if Middle East inflation persists. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh in first week. ECB hawks (Villeroy de Galhau, Schnabel) signaling June rate hike. 10Y Treasury selling off, yields rising on inflation fears.

factors.name: Bond selloff & real yields
factors.summary: Bond market 'rocked by violent selloff' per MarketWatch. Real rates rising as inflation expectations climb from Iran energy shock. Long-duration growth stocks (Nasdaq 100 core holdings) highly sensitive to real yield expansion.

factors.name: Energy shock, inflation re-pricing
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz closure risks; Brent oil volatility; Piper Sandler warns of summer oil highs. Eurozone CPI at 3% per ECB. Inflation expectations ticking up, crowding out near-term rate cut hopes and pressuring tech multiples.

factors.name: Dollar strength (inverse for tech)
factors.summary: USD index strengthening on war risk re-pricing and higher rates. TD Securities still bearish dollar, but near-term bounce threatens Nasdaq's international revenue translation. Gold down >1.6% (XAU/USD below $4,500) on USD strength.

factors.name: Consumer sentiment weakness
factors.summary: Survey shows Americans feel worse than ever about finances; Gen Z driving discounter gains (Walmart, Ross). Pressure on premium retail and auto (AutoZone worst day in 4 years despite beating estimates). Margin compression concerns from inflation/fuel costs.

factors.name: Earnings growth sustainability questions
factors.summary: MarketWatch editorial: 'Double-digit earnings growth won't stop next bear market.' Spiking S&P profits often signal final innings of bull market; history suggests stocks 'on thin ice.' Valuation concern amid multiple compression risk.

factors.name: Recent price action (4h/24h context)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.35% in 4h, +0.02% in 24h. Minimal net movement despite strong close yesterday. Price at 30,001 near prior highs but lacking conviction follow-through intra-day. Suggests caution on momentum-chasing.

factors.name: Bias history - no reversal signal
factors.summary: Three consecutive NEUTRAL 5/10 reads (09:54, 16:43, 19:22 UTC May 26). No prior bullish confirmation triggered. Current move may be early-stage, lacks multi-timeframe confluence.

key_levels.resistance: 30,100–30,200 (prior May high area; record close 26,656 on NQ equiv ~30,030)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is attempting to extend its record-setting rally on AI-driven mega-cap strength (Micron +20.8%), defense/space contractor wins (SpaceX, Raytheon, Lockheed), and broad-based risk-on breadth (S&P, Russell 2000 records). However, the move is being choked by rising inflation expectations from Middle East energy shock (Strait of Hormuz closure risk, Brent >3% up), which has triggered talk of Fed rate hike series from Kashkari and ECB hawks signaling June tightening. Bond yields are accelerating upward, real rates expanding, and the US Dollar is strengthening—all headwinds for long-duration growth multiples. Consumer sentiment is weakening, and analysts warn that earnings growth alone may not sustain valuations much further. The index is at record territory but lacking conviction follow-through intra-day, and prior bias reads (3x NEUTRAL) suggest no confirmed reversal.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first full week agenda / FOMC communications (rate hike odds tracking inflation data)
Australia CPI (May 28, 01:30 GMT) — geopolitical inflation gauge
Bank of Japan policy decision & BoJ Governor Ueda comments (May 27) — yen carry trade implications
US economic data: NFP (early June), PCE inflation (late May/early June) — critical for Fed re-pricing
Tech earnings season phase 2: Apple WWDC (June 8) AI showcase; MSFT/GOOGL cloud capex guidance
SpaceX IPO launch (mid-June, ~June 13 roadshow) — high-profile listing event
Oil/energy prices: Strait of Hormuz closure duration & US-Iran ceasefire status
screening.risk_warning: A sustained US-Iran ceasefire would defang energy/inflation concerns, allowing equities to re-rate higher on AI upside. Conversely, military escalation (Israel-Lebanon, US-Iran direct conflict) could crash risk assets and force Fed tightening, triggering >2% Nasdaq selloff. Bond yield spike and real rate expansion are the single biggest risk to the thesis—if 10Y hits 4.5%+ and TIPS real yield crosses +1.5%, tech multiples face severe compression. Early bias reads (NEUTRAL) and weak intra-day follow-through suggest this rally lacks confirmation; chasing at 30,100+ risks mean-reversion pull-back to 29,800.
screening.entry_guidance: Move has not extended significantly (only +0.35% 4h, +0.02% 24h), but bond yield backdrop is hostile and bias history shows no prior confirmation. Wait for either: (1) pullback to 29,900–29,950 support for lower-risk long entry, or (2) clear break & close above 30,150 with breadth confirmation before chasing rally. Given Fed/ECB hawkish rhetoric and inflation re-pricing, reduce position size if entering above 30,050.
